news.title: UK homebuilder Bellway warns of slowing demand, rising energy costs
news.para: British homebuilder Bellway reported a cooling in customer demand and rising building costs due to higher energy prices, despite maintaining annual profit and volume targets. Soaring energy prices have impacted input materials, affecting UK builders and government housing targets. The company's private reservation rate fell, though incentives helped mitigate the impact.

news.title: Odisha cabined approves reduction in stamp duty on houses for EWS
news.para: Odisha's state cabinet has significantly reduced stamp duty and registration fees for Economically Weaker Section beneficiaries of PMAY-U 2.0 and state housing schemes. Charges will now range from 0.6% to 0.7%, a substantial drop from the current 6-7%, aiming to boost housing uptake and streamline the Centre's urban housing program.

news.title: ED conducts fresh raids in Punjab, Uttar Pradesh in Sanjeev Arora case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has conducted fresh searches in a money laundering probe related to an alleged Rs 100 crore GST fraud. The investigation involves Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora and others, with searches spanning multiple locations in Punjab and Uttar Pradesh. Arora was arrested last month and is currently in judicial custody.

news.title: Construction material prices surge, Chennai developers warn of costlier homes
news.para: Construction material costs have surged over 23%, potentially increasing house prices by 10% due to the West Asian conflict and a supply crunch from quarry closures. Industry stakeholders warn this could be passed on to homebuyers, impacting sales and jobs. Developers are urging the government to ensure uninterrupted supply of legally sourced materials and regulate prices.

news.title: Ten years on, Telangana’s Lifts Act still stuck in limbo
news.para: Telangana is yet to enforce its Lifts Act, despite a decade-old proposal and recent fatal accidents. The High Court has questioned the delay, while experts highlight the need for uniform safety standards to prevent tragedies, especially for vulnerable groups. The proposed act aims to regulate installations, maintenance, and inspections, mirroring regulations in ten other Indian states.

news.title: After 32 years, VDA to launch three mega townships in Varanasi
news.para: Varanasi Development Authority is launching three integrated townships, including a Sports City, marking its first major initiative since 1994. Backed by Rs 400 crore seed capital, these projects aim to boost planned urban development. The land-pooling model is being adopted, transforming farmers into development partners.

news.title: Bengaluru: Bescom to extend OC relief to buildings completed before May 31
news.para: Bescom has eased occupancy certificate (OC) norms for residential buildings up to 2,400 sqft in Greater Bengaluru, allowing permanent electricity connections without an OC. This move clarifies confusion over a previous deadline, ensuring homeowners who completed construction or had temporary connections before May 31, 2026, are eligible for the exemption.

news.title: TCS renews 1.5 million sq ft Chennai office lease in ₹1,420-crore rental deal
news.para: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has renewed its lease for nearly 15 lakh sq ft of office space in Chennai for 10 years, committing approximately Rs 1,420 crore. This significant renewal underscores TCS's long-term commitment to Chennai as a key delivery hub, despite ongoing discussions about AI's impact on tech employment.

news.title: Delhi: MCD demolishes, partially razes 26 properties in crackdown against non-compliance
news.para: Authorities are intensifying action against illegal constructions and safety lapses across Delhi following recent tragedies.  The MCD has demolished and sealed numerous properties, while inspections revealed widespread fire safety and building norm violations in hotels, guest houses, and commercial establishments.  Strict enforcement continues under various master plans and acts.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government to simplify law governing RERA
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has directed officials to simplify legal provisions for corporate restructuring, LLPs, and RERA real estate transactions to align with economic needs. He emphasized making the Stamp and Registration Department transparent, technology-driven, and citizen-centric, akin to passport service centers, to enhance ease of doing business and investor confidence.

news.title: PLOTRIX: How Kaushall Prakash is rewriting the real estate development playbook through the DM model
news.para: India's real estate industry has long struggled with flawed execution models, leaving landowners with limited options. PLOTRIX introduces a Development & Marketing Management (DM) Model, offering a structured, accountable framework for end-to-end project execution. This innovative approach aims to maximize value and transparency, addressing the industry's core challenges.

news.title: Builder-bank nexus case: CBI files charge sheet against Ninex Developers, its director
news.para: The CBI has filed a charge sheet against Ninex Developers Ltd and its director for alleged fraudulent activities in a Gurugram housing project. This marks the CBI's 11th report in builder-banker nexus cases, where thousands of homebuyers were reportedly cheated through deceptive promises and misleading representations.

news.title: SC directs execution of settlement in Ardee group family dispute
news.para: The Supreme Court has ordered the execution of a settlement in a property dispute concerning the Ardee Group and family assets of the late Ashok Varma. Daughters Shefali Varma and Shibani Varma Kapur have reached an amicable resolution through mediation facilitated by former Supreme Court judge Justice Kurian Joseph. The court has directed parties to implement the agreed terms.

news.title: Polish residential developer Robyg plans Warsaw IPO
news.para: Polish developer Robyg plans an IPO on the Warsaw Stock Exchange, aiming to raise approximately 400 million zlotys ($109 million) for development and land acquisition.  The company's owner, TAG Beteiligungs- und Immobilienverwaltungs, will sell a portion of its shares but retain majority control. Robyg intends to allocate 10% of shares to retail investors.

news.title: Smartworks lease out over 400 seats to Japanese NBFC subsidiary in Mumbai
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces has secured a significant deal, leasing over 400 seats to a Japanese NBFC subsidiary in Mumbai for a five-year term. This agreement is projected to generate ₹35 crore in rental revenue, highlighting the company's success in catering to large enterprise clients who constitute a substantial portion of its income.

news.title: SC-appointed amicus curiae seeks direction to MCD on action taken against illegal constructions
news.para: Following a deadly building collapse in Delhi, an amicus curiae has urged the Supreme Court to direct the MCD to survey and act against illegal constructions. The court is asked to mandate structural audits, sealing, and demolition of unauthorized structures, and investigate how the collapsed building was allowed to continue construction despite multiple bookings.

news.title: Bihar government set to launch paperless property registration system
news.para: Bihar is ushering in a fully paperless property registration system, a significant digital governance reform. This initiative, approved by the cabinet, aims to boost transparency and efficiency in land transactions. Buyers and sellers will soon complete the entire process online using digital signatures and Aadhaar authentication, eliminating the need for physical documents. The phased rollout begins this month.

news.title: Karnataka government provides housing to 427 families in Madikeri
news.para: The Karnataka government is providing housing facilities to the poorest citizens. Eligible families in Madikeri taluk are receiving documents for homes under the Basava and Ambedkar Housing Schemes. The district has received funds for house construction. In Virajpet constituency, 527 houses are planned. The first phase will shelter 427 families.

news.title: Arnya Realestates Fund invests over ₹1,000 crore across 11 housing projects
news.para: Arnya’s debt strategy is expected to reach a total deployment commitment of Rs 1,200 crore, including direct investments, by the time of its final close, which is targeted for June end. The firm is also planning to launch the second series of its debt strategy during the second quarter of the current financial year.

news.title: RWA federation seeks fire safety audit of hotels, PGs and highrises in Noida
news.para: Following recent fire incidents, the Federation of Noida Residents Welfare Associations has requested the GB Nagar police commissioner to strengthen fire safety standards. They are demanding inspections of hotels, guest houses, and paying guest accommodations. The federation also seeks verification of Fire No Objection Certificates in highrise buildings and action against illegal constructions.
